title: RegionIndicatorMessage
description: One indicator message published by a worker to RabbitMQ on routing key country.{CODE}.region. It carries a SINGLE theme's indicators for one region at one period. The backend upserts it into `indicators.<theme>` of the matching `snapshots` document (it never overwrites the whole document). This is the contract between workers (Python) and the backend (NestJS). `level`, `period`, `theme`, and indicator keys are opaque strings — the backend never branches on their values.

country_code.description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country code, e.g. BR, US, AR

level.description: Administrative division level name, e.g. UF or municipio for Brazil. Opaque to the backend — supplied by the worker.

code.description: Region code at this level, e.g. 35 (IBGE code for São Paulo)

parent_code.description: Parent region code (e.g. the UF code for a municipio), or null at the top level

period.description: Reference period as a STRING (never a Date), e.g. 2022 or 2023-P1. First-class so adding a year is an insert, not a migration.

theme.description: Which of the four themes this message's indicators belong to

source.description: Primary data source for this theme block, e.g. IBGE, SICONFI, DataSUS

fetched_at.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp of when the data was fetched

indicators.description: Map of indicator key to IndicatorValue. Keys must come from the vocabulary in CLAUDE.md section 13.

unit.description: Unit of measurement, e.g. people, %, R$, MW, beds/100k

source.description: Specific source citation, e.g. IBGE Census 2022
